title: Gottman Method Therapy

intro.heading: The Benefits of Gottman Therapy for Couples
intro.body: Gottman Therapy is a research-based approach to couples counseling, developed by Drs. John and Julie Gottman. It is rooted in the extensive study of real-life couples over several decades, making it one of the most scientifically-validated forms of relationship therapy. This method is designed to help couples build stronger, healthier relationships, by understanding and improving their emotional and communication dynamics.

features.title: Mindfulness
features.desc: At the heart of DBT is mindfulness, which teaches individuals to be fully present in the moment. This skill helps in recognizing and coping with intense emotions without being overwhelmed.

features.title: Emotion Regulation
features.desc: DBT teaches skills to manage and change intense emotions that are causing problems in a person’s life.

features.title: Interpersonal Effectiveness
features.desc: This component involves techniques that enable individuals to communicate with others in a way that is assertive, maintains self-respect, and strengthens relationships.

features.title: Distress Tolerance
features.desc: This aspect focuses on increasing a person’s tolerance of negative emotion, rather than trying to escape from it. It includes coping strategies like distraction, self-soothing, and improving the moment.

features.title: Reducing Risky Behavior
features.desc: DBT has been proven particularly effective in reducing self-harming behaviors and suicidal thoughts, which are often associated with BPD.

features.title: Decreases Interfering Behaviors
features.desc: DBT addresses issues like missing sessions or being unengaged in therapy, making the therapeutic process more effective.

features.title: Building Life Skills
features.desc: Beyond treating specific disorders, DBT imparts valuable life skills that contribute to overall well-being and resilience.

features.title: Adaptive
features.desc: DBT's versatility makes it effective for a wide range of disorders and age groups, including adolescents.

features.title: Growth Tools
features.desc: DBT has a structure and can be intensive. It requires learning new skills as part of therapy.

cta.heading: DBT is a structured, intensive form of therapy that requires a commitment to learning and applying new skills. Its effectiveness is supported by a substantial body of research, making it a trusted choice for both therapists and individuals seeking to overcome complex emotional and behavioral issues. Through DBT, individuals can learn to manage their emotions more effectively, navigate interpersonal relationships skillfully, and build a life worth living.
cta.body: Your therapist will carefully explain and tailor treatment methods in your plan, ensuring they align with your specific needs and therapeutic goals.

excerpt: A research-based approach to couples counseling that helps partners communicate, resolve conflict, and build deeper emotional intimacy.
seoDescription: Gottman Method Therapy in Philadelphia: an evidence-based, culturally-affirming approach to couples counseling that strengthens communication and connection.
